mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving upward corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized during high inflation periods signals capacity utilization bottlenecks, prompting monetary tightening fears and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day returns over rolling three-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds four percent, real rates turn positive, tightening financial conditions and triggering immediate equity depreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX five-day returns are positive in >70% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds four percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates inventory misalignment; when retail sales outpace GDP growth, earnings surprises drive positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Retail sales outpacing GDP yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production declines signal immediate supply chain disruptions and reduced manufacturing output, causing short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases.
